bead.title: Stage 4.19: dashboard /nodes/:nodeId/providers page (agent-routing visibility)
bead.description:
Add a new Svelte page at src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/providers/+page.svelte under the SvelteKit dashboard. Shows live provider state for the running ddx-server: for each configured provider, display name, type, URL, configured or auto-selected model, live status (connected / unreachable / HTTP error), and model count. Mirror the output of `ddx agent providers` from the CLI.

Data source: a GraphQL query `providers: [ProviderStatus!]!` (or similar) that the Go server resolver implements via the new Visibility 3 `ddx agent providers` code path (import the same Go helpers used by the CLI command, do not duplicate logic). Subscription for live status updates is out of scope for this bead — add it later when we have a reason to burn the websocket budget on it.

Also add a "current route for --model-ref" widget showing which provider/model the default profile currently resolves to, so the operator can see "if I run ddx agent run --model-ref code-medium right now, it goes here." Pulls from the Visibility 6 route-status data.

Dependencies: blocked on Svelte migration Stage 3 (scaffold) and on Visibility 3, 5, 6 from the agent-routing epic.
bead.acceptance: (1) New route /nodes/:nodeId/providers renders under the Svelte dashboard. (2) Table shows each configured provider with name/type/URL/model/status/model-count matching `ddx agent providers`. (3) "Current route for default profile" widget shows which provider would be selected for a default execute-bead call. (4) Manual: start `ddx server`, open `/nodes/:nodeId/providers`, verify the live data matches `ddx agent providers` in a terminal. (5) Playwright test: page loads, provider rows render, table has expected columns.
